text: The surgeon begins the procedure by dividing the lesser omentum, revealing the caudate lobe of the liver. As the omental tissue is divided upwards, the right crus of the diaphragm becomes visible. The area around the crus is cleared in preparation for placing a holding suture.

The surgeon places a traction suture over the xiphoid process using a straight long needle, suturing it to the crux of the diaphragm. The suture is passed underneath the costal margin, enabling retraction of the liver upwards.
